# FinOps Framework Changelog: 2025 → 2026

> Source: [finops.org/insights/2026-finops-framework](https://www.finops.org/insights/2026-finops-framework/)

---

## Definition Change

| | Definition |
|---|---|
| **2025** | "...maximizes the business value of **cloud**..." |
| **2026** | "...maximizes the business value of **technology**..." |

FinOps Foundation mission also updated: "Advancing the People who manage the Value of Technology" (previously cloud-focused).

---

## Renamed Capabilities (6)

| 2025 Name | 2026 Name | Domain | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Workload Optimization | **Usage Optimization** | Optimize Usage & Cost | Applies across all scopes, not just cloud workloads |
| Policy & Governance | **Governance, Policy & Risk** | Manage the FinOps Practice | Accounts for full range of technology categories and intersecting disciplines |
| FinOps Tools & Services | **Automation, Tools & Services** | Manage the FinOps Practice | Addresses automation across all technology categories |
| Benchmarking | **KPI & Benchmarking** | Quantify Business Value | Metrics aligned to specific technology categories and business context |
| Architecting for Cloud | **Architecting & Workload Placement** | Optimize Usage & Cost | Multiple technology categories in placement decisions |
| Cloud Sustainability | **Sustainability** | Optimize Usage & Cost | Applies beyond public cloud |

All six received substantial content updates replacing cloud-specific references with technology-inclusive language.

---

## New Capability (1)

**Executive Strategy Alignment** — added to Manage the FinOps Practice domain.

Connects technology spend, usage, and adoption to business strategy. Four sub-areas:
1. Executive Priority Alignment
2. Multi-Year Investment Strategy
3. Facilitate Product Prioritization Strategy
4. Enable Strategic Decision Support

---

## Merged Capability (1)

**Onboarding Workloads** → merged into **Architecting & Workload Placement**

The new capability covers both architectural decisions and workload onboarding/placement across technology categories. The Onboarding Workloads page (finops.org/framework/capabilities/onboarding-workloads/) no longer exists.

---

## New Concepts

### Technology Categories (7)
Entirely new framework section. Categories represent types of technology organizations consume:
1. Public Cloud
2. SaaS
3. Data Center
4. Data Cloud Platforms
5. AI
6. Private Cloud
7. Licenses

Categories = "what" (technical domain). Scopes = "why" (business context).

### Updated Scopes
Redefined as "a defined segment of spending across technology categories, aligned to business constructs." Key additions:
- Business-driven initiation
- Lifecycle guidance (evolution, conclusion, overlap management)
- Governance principles: few, simple, purposeful

---

## Enhanced Content

### Intersecting Disciplines
Enhanced to address coordination with allied disciplines:
- ITAM, SAM, ITFM, Security, Sustainability, Enterprise Architecture

### Personas
- New "Allied Personas" framing: ITAM, ITFM, ITSM, Security, Sustainability
- New "FinOps Enabled Executive" concept (C-1 level: VP/SVP/EVP)

---

## Unchanged

- **4 Domain names** — same names, but now technology-agnostic in scope
- **Maturity model** — Crawl, Walk, Run
- **6 Principles** — unchanged
- **Core personas** — FinOps Practitioner, Engineering, Finance, Leadership, Procurement, Product
- **Planning & Estimating** and **Forecasting** — remain separate capabilities
- **FinOps Lifecycle** — Inform, Optimize, Operate phases

---

## Net Capability Count

| | Count |
|---|---|
| 2025 capabilities | 23 |
| Removed (merged) | -1 (Onboarding Workloads) |
| Added | +1 (Executive Strategy Alignment) |
| Renamed | 6 (no net change) |
| **2026 total** | **22** |

Note: The 2025 count of 23 includes the capability names as listed on finops.org prior to the 2026 update: Allocation, Anomaly Management, Architecting for Cloud, Benchmarking, Budgeting, Cloud Sustainability, Data Ingestion, FinOps Assessment, FinOps Education & Enablement, FinOps Practice Operations, FinOps Tools & Services, Forecasting, Intersecting Disciplines, Invoicing & Chargeback, Licensing & SaaS, Onboarding Workloads, Planning & Estimating, Policy & Governance, Rate Optimization, Reporting & Analytics, Unit Economics, Workload Optimization, and (added mid-cycle or at release) Executive Strategy Alignment predecessor concepts.
